summary: Iter5 — Firebase RTDB adapter wiring validated end-to-end. Backend 39/39 pytest still pass (no regression). Frontend Firebase wiring works exactly as designed: dropdown shows 'Firebase Realtime DB', description text varies by mode, selecting Firebase + Start session attaches FirebaseAdapter without crashing the app. As predicted (Anonymous auth/RTDB rules not yet configured on chilleasy-842fc), ensureAuth() rejects and the adapter sets linkStatus={status:'error'} → chip renders 'ERROR' gracefully. Dashboard remains fully usable, no unhandled JS error, no console errors at all. publishCommand path verified: clicking cmd-setpoint-btn while in Firebase mode (started, but auth-failed) produces a clean toast 'Command failed: setpoint' (push() rejects, .catch returns false) without crashing. Auth flow (QA login → JWT → dashboard) and Simulator regression both intact.

integration_issues.issue: MOCKED — could not assert real RTDB read/write because Anonymous sign-in is not enabled on Firebase project chilleasy-842fc (expected per problem statement). Test verified the GRACEFUL FAILURE path only: ensureAuth rejects → linkStatus 'ERROR', no crash, toast on failed publishCommand.

action_items: (User action, not code) Enable Anonymous sign-in in Firebase Console → Authentication → Sign-in method, AND set RTDB rules to allow auth!=null read/write under /devices, before expecting live telemetry from FirebaseAdapter.
(Optional, low priority) firebaseClient.js: empty catch on `} catch {}` lines 282-283 of dataSource.js — fine as-is (cleanup), but consider logging in dev.

(Optional) FirebaseAdapter.start() does not return any indication of auth success to the caller; UI relies entirely on linkStatus listener — current design is OK because sendCommand is gated by `running`, but documenting the contract would help.
critical_code_review_comments: firebaseClient.ensureAuth() caches the promise in module-scope `_ready`. If the first sign-in REJECTS (current state for chilleasy-842fc), every subsequent ensureAuth() call returns the SAME rejected promise — meaning even after the user enables Anonymous auth in Firebase Console, the app must be hard-reloaded. Recommend: on rejection, null out `_ready` so the next call retries. One-line fix in lib/firebaseClient.js.

FirebaseAdapter.start() optimistically sets status to 'connected' (line 267 of dataSource.js) AFTER attaching listeners but BEFORE any data has actually been received. Combined with the statusRef onValue overriding it later, this is mostly harmless, but a fresh project with no /devices/{id}/status node will leave the chip showing 'CONNECTED · firebase-rtdb' indefinitely without any actual telemetry — could confuse users. Consider waiting for first onValue snapshot before flipping to 'connected'.

publishCommand uses `serverTimestamp()` in the value object — this is correct, but it means the local cmd object will not have a usable `ts` field until the server roundtrip; UI code that reads back `ts` synchronously would see the sentinel object. Not currently consumed in UI, so no bug, but worth noting for future code that reads cmd queue.
package.json: firebase@12.x is correctly added; bundle size impact ~150KB gzipped — no action needed but worth tracking.

Observability: when ensureAuth rejects with auth/admin-restricted-operation (Anonymous disabled) the error string surfaces in the chip but is truncated. Consider logging full error to console for debug visibility.

success_rate.backend: 100% (33 passed, 6 skipped, 0 failed — same 39 totals as iter4)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on the wiring + graceful-failure assertions defined in this iteration's scope

seed_data_creation: None this iteration (no new test data created beyond what iter4 already produced).
context_for_next_testing_agent: FirebaseAdapter wiring is complete and behaves correctly under both happy-path-blocked (current state — Anonymous auth disabled) and partial-success conditions. To assert RTDB end-to-end in a future iteration: (1) user enables Anonymous sign-in in Firebase Console for project chilleasy-842fc, (2) sets RTDB rules to {"rules":{".read":"auth!=null",".write":"auth!=null"}}, (3) seed devices/vx-001/telemetry/live with a valid telemetry object via Firebase REST API or console. The ensureAuth promise-cache caveat (see code review #1) means a hard reload is required after enabling auth — recommend fixing that one-liner before next Firebase test pass. Backend is unchanged from iter4. Test reproduction: `cd /app && export $(grep -v '^#' frontend/.env | xargs -d'\n') && python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Frontend Playwright: log in with qa@vaxchain.test, click Settings tab, open settings-source select, select 'Firebase Realtime DB', click start-session-btn, assert device-link-status text contains 'ERROR' (or 'CONNECTED' if auth is now enabled), assert no console errors.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Firebase ERROR chip is the expected and correct surfacing of 'auth/admin-restricted-operation' from signInAnonymously when Anonymous provider is disabled. The FirebaseAdapter handles it gracefully via a try/catch around ensureAuth, sets linkStatus to error, and prevents the rest of the start() flow from running. publishCommand similarly catches push() rejection and returns false → useSession.sendCommand shows toast.error.
